transcript.text: Hi Alice.
Hey, hi.
Hey, how are you doing?
Good.
How about you?
Good as well.
mean, busy with so much documentation for onboarding, for sure.
Oh, really?
Yes.
You know, Bridget, thank you.
Go ahead.
Bridget, tell me about you, that you've been in the company for almost a year.
Yeah, I joined the company last year around July.
I joined in early July.
So I've been here for like 10 months, 10 months right now.
Oh, wow.
Yeah, almost a year.
And how has it been?
Yeah, it's been a great experience.
Tell me about yourself, your background.
Anything you'd like to know?
Well, I studied industrial engineering, and then I worked as an executive assistant, operations assistant, and HR recruiter for a while.
So I've been doing this for three years.
But I've been learning about automations and coding in the last few months.
So that's why I'm working with Jason.
I'm his EA, and I'll be helping him, you know, have executive assistant and have automations and workflows.
But right now, I'm helping Marcel, too.
So I'm hoping to learn about marketing and things.
That's awesome.
You must be so smart.
But I've been in the executive industry for just one year.
I didn't, I wasn't doing, I wasn't an EA before.
My background is in economics and finance.
But then I started this role last year, so I don't have that much experience compared to you.
Interesting.
But you do have a lot of experience in growthx.
mean, you know the company way more than you.
Yeah, me too.
Do you have any questions for me, anything you'd like me to help you with?
Well, I was told that I should contact an onboarding buddy, and Rich told me that you will be great for that.
So she told me about meeting me regularly to talk about onboarding, I guess, but I'm not sure exactly what it will be, just for questions, I guess.
Yeah, she pinged me last night, and then she told me that I'm going to be your onboarding buddy, and I was like, what's an onboarding?
So she told me that basically to answer any question that you have, anything that you need help with, so you can just come to me, ping me anytime, and ask me about it, and I'll be happy to help.
Awesome.
Tell me more about what you do, so I understand a bit about your context and growthx.
Okay.

So right now, I've been supporting Marcel, but then I'm transitioning to supporting Daniel, because right now, Marcel needs someone who's in person, because we have a new office, so he wants someone who can be going to the office to help around.
Whenever visitors come into the office, can find someone to help him with that.
So right now, I'm transitioning to supporting Daniel.
Have you met Daniel?
Not yet, but I know that he is the other co-founder.
Yeah.
They are the co-founder.
So right now I'm transitioning to supporting him.
And I've also been helping around with invoices, sending contracts, managing the CRM, sales, follow-ups, a bunch of other stuff.
But because I joined the company when we were only three employees.
So I used to do a lot.
But right now it's good because many people have joined and then they've taken quite a lot of stuff from my plate.
Okay, I see.
Wow, that's so cool that you were in the first three people.
How was that like in the beginning?
It's been a journey.
Like the work overload was so overwhelming.
And then when Lucille announced the company first in LinkedIn, the demand grew.
And so I had a lot to do.
Like I had, I had.

I was doing hiring, was doing sales, was doing invoices, I was doing follow-ups, like basically everything plus coordination, I have to coordinate myself, meetings, it was just crazy, but yeah, it's been a good journey of growth and learning, because when I started, I didn't know how to use some of the tools, but right now I know how to use them, like our CRM RTO, I don't know whether you have access to that.
What is the name again?
RTO, let me write it in the chat.
Sure, yeah, was wondering if it was CRM, but I don't know.

Yeah, it's something that I've never used before, so I came to the company, I found that this is what they use, so I had to learn it, so it's been awesome, it's been a great journey, I've learned a lot.
Did you send the name in this chat, in the Zoom meeting?
Yeah, yeah, yeah, in the chat.
It's so weird, I don't see it.
You don't see it?
No, I didn't get any.
Okay, let me just share my screen and then I can show you if that's easier.
Can you see my screen?
Yeah, I do.
Okay.
Add you.
Yeah, so this is how it looks like.
And whenever you need access to anything that you don't have, you can just let me know and I can add you.
So I can add you to add you right now.
Do you need access to this though?
I just got to know it.
I didn't know we're using it.
So probably not yet.
I mean, I'm just using a linear notion.
Okay.
Yeah, but in case you...
Access, you can just let me know.
So how are you going to be helping Jason?
Well, for now, it is helping him focus on things that really matter for him and organizing his workloads.
So I'm managing his email, calendar, and besides that, as an executive assistant, I will also be helping to look into the workflows that he has.
So anything that is connected between Zapier, Notion, see how we can improve those and make sure that he is on the run very well.
But with Marcel, it's very different.
This just came up yesterday.
He wanted me to help with LinkedIn because he has a lot of traffic in there, a lot of comments and things.
And he wants to interact with that.
So I'm going to I'm going to find.
and what we can do to make it easier for everyone.
That's the overview so far.
Yeah, that's awesome.
It's really helpful because when I started, we didn't have any automations.
Or maybe we had one or two, so everything was manual.
So it was crazy.
Yeah, so that's going to be really helpful.
I'm sure.
You said that you were located where?
Kenya, Nairobi, Kenya.
Oh, that's so cool.
What time is there?
It's there at the moment.
Yeah, it's 9 p.m.
right now.
And where are you based?
Colombia, Medellin.
Awesome.
So the guy that joined the sales team that I was training yesterday is also from Colombia.
I don't know whether you met him.
Owen.
Owen.
Yes, I did.
We had an onboarding call.
Yesterday and he mentioned that he's here too.
was like, oh, we should meet someday for, you know, co-work or something.
But yeah, hopefully we will get to that.
That is awesome.
So nice to meet you and to work with you as well.
Yeah.
Well, so far, I think I don't have...
Oh, yeah, one thing.
Do you have a signature in your email from Voguex set up?
I do, but then I created it myself.
It's not something that is...
It's not standardized in the company.
It's something that I created myself.
Let me show you how it looks like.
Oh, wow.
It's not standardized.
Interesting.
Yeah.
So this is what is...
It's so simple.
I'm the one who created this.
Can you see my screen?
Yeah.
Yeah, I do love it.
Can you send me an email, please?
And I'll copy that for my...
Okay.
It's lovely.
I like that it's simple and to the point.
Is it?
Yes.
So, thank you so much.
And let's see what else.
I don't really have any other blockers so far.
I guess if you can just give me advice on anything, like in the way, let's say I could interact with Marcel, for example, or in general with the company.
Okay, with Marcel, Marcel is a really cool guy.
You met him yesterday, so I know you've seen that.
Though he prefers async communication.
Like, he doesn't like too much meetings.
If there's something, you can text him.
If it's something, you can use Slack, but then if it's urgent, you can use WhatsApp for anything that's urgent.
So he likes async communication, but then he also likes someone who asks questions.
Whenever you're stuck, just go direct to him and ask him.
But then it's better if you know the boundary between something that you need to ask and something that you can research or ask someone else before getting to him.
What else about myself?
Actually, I can't think of anything else.
For everyone else, like, most of these people are new.
So the person that I started with is Sadira and Jimmy, but then Jimmy left.
So it's only one person that I've been interested.

acting with quite a lot and Bridget for like some few months right now yeah but everything everyone is cool um we use slack communication mostly we have different slack channels i'm sure you've already added to the slacks right yeah that's right yeah yeah so like there's no like rules there's no like any standard reference communication preferences just be yourself in case you're still just be open go to the slack channel and ask and so they prefer open communication if it's better you try to avoid dms as much as possible and use the public channels just for transparency yes um have you ever met anyone in the company in person no i'm in kenya i'm the only person in kenya so um Bridget was you
For her honeymoon.
And that was when she was being on boarded in the company.
I didn't know her yet.
So I got to know she was in Kenya when she already left.
So I didn't get to meet her.
Cool.
And do you like to use Slack or WhatsApp more, personally?
Personally, I like WhatsApp.
then if it's work-related, I'm always on Slack.
But then if you can't find me because of time zone differences, you can always WhatsApp me.
Yeah.
I can share my number.
Let's see.
Let's see if I can see it in your profile.
Is it there?
No, it's not.
Let me type it in the chat and then let me know if you can see it or not.
Okay.
Can you see it?
It's so weird.
I don't see any messages being sent through this call.
Isn't that weird?
Oh, I know where the problem is.
It's because I'm sending it to your note taker instead of...
Instead of me?
Yeah.
Now, yeah.
Okay, gotcha.
You know, Teka has the same name, so I didn't know.
Makes sense.
All right.
What else in general?
I'm just curious, like, what do you like to do in general, you know, with your free time?
That's a good question, but I'm a boring person.
So, like...
I'm sure not.
I'm quite a boring person.
I like to listen to music a lot, a lot.
And then I also do hairdressing and makeup.
I love to do that.
And some DIY stuff.
And I'm also a mom.
So most of my free time, I'm always with my children, just spending time going out, maybe going to the park, you know.
Yeah.
What else do I do?
How many children do you have?
My life is boring.
What?
How many children do you have?
I have three.
Oh, wow.
So cool.
You always wanted to have a big family?
No, I'm done.
It's crazy.
I'm done.
And then I'm also doing my master's right now.
So it's really.
Difficult, balancing, studying, working, it's too much, like...
You look so young, like, how old are you, 20?
How old do I look?
I just said that, 20, you look so young.
I'm 28, not so far from 28.
How about you?
Wow, awesome.
So who do you live with?
Do you have any savings, anything, like, any fun fact about you?
I'm an only child and I live by myself.
Yeah, right now, yes.
But I do martial arts.
I mean, that's not fun, but that's interesting.
There's one one.
Yeah.
What type of martial arts is it?
I haven't heard of that before.
That must be awesome.
So how is it like being an only child?
How is the experience?
Do ever wish you had siblings or you're just okay?
It's so boring to be an only child.
I would have preferred to have at least a brother, sister, whatever, at least another person there with me.
My mom wanted the same, my parents wanted the same, but they couldn't have health issues, so they couldn't have another kid.
But it's very boring.
You don't have the default person to play with.
And it's kind of lonely too, because sometimes you don't relate with your parents.
You cannot tell them everything.
So sometimes I feel nice to have that body that is there all the time.
Yeah.
Yeah, I understand.
I also…
I I had a sister because I'm an only girl in my family, though I have two brothers.
There are three siblings at home.
And then, like, my question is, has that affected your personality?
Are you an introvert?
Are you extroverted?
Do you like to socialize?
I mean, that probably affected me, yes, for sure.
I spent a lot of time alone at home.
So I hide it on books, basically.
At I spent my time reading and just being a good student.
So that made me an introvert.
I was very introverted in school.
But then in college, I, you know, I had to be more social.
So right now, I'm in the middle, you know, like middle but more introverted, I will say.
How about you?
Same.
I'm called, I'm by that, I think.
I'm in between.
I'm not an introvert.
I'm not an extrovert.
So I keep paying.
It depends on the kind of energy that you bring.
I'll be able to reciprocate that.
If I meet you, you're quiet.
I'll also be quiet.
But then if I meet you and you like to talk, you have good vibes.
I'll also match your energy.
Yeah.
Exactly.
Oh, well, I would like to meet you someday.
mean, growthx hasn't thought about having off-sites?
Yeah, I think it's in the plan.
They're planning about having annual events where employees can meet.
But then it's not something that has been established.
So I can't give you a very solid answer right now.
Because it's something that they were thinking about.
But it hasn't been decided on yet.
Yeah.
Yeah.
mean, the company is so young, I bet.
But yeah.
But I guess at some point...
The ones that meet are the leaders, the directors, the VPs.
They had an offsite like two weeks ago and it was the very first one.
So it's progress, you know.
Yes, it is.
Cool.
I don't think it makes sense for us to meet regularly.
what do you think?
We can just meet whenever I have a question, I guess.
Or if I can ask, I'll do it.
Yeah, so you can just ping me.
Anytime you have a question, can just text me.
If it's something that I require to share my screen or you require to share your screen, then we can always meet.
But then it's not necessary.
You can just use Slack or WhatsApp.
Sounds perfect.
Well, thank you so much.
It was so nice meeting you.
Thanks.
Have a great day.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory meeting between Dignory Carmona (new hire) and Alice Ochieng (onboarding buddy) to discuss roles, company culture, and establish a working relationship.](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=4.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Alice (10 months at growthx.ai) will be Dignory's onboarding buddy, available for questions and support](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
  - [Dignory joins as EA for Jason and Marcel, focusing on email/calendar management, workflow optimization, and LinkedIn engagement](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [growthx.ai is a rapidly growing startup (from 3 to multiple employees in 10 months) with a preference for async communication and transparency](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)

## Topics

### Company Growth and Culture

  - [growthx.ai expanded from 3 to multiple employees in 10 months](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=254.0)
  - [Preference for async communication (Slack, email) with WhatsApp for urgency](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
  - [Open communication encouraged; public Slack channels preferred over DMs](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=679.0)
  - [No standardized email signatures; Alice created her own simple version](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)
  - [Annual employee meetups being considered, not yet established](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)

### Roles and Responsibilities

  - [Dignory: EA for Jason (email/calendar management, workflow optimization) and Marcel (LinkedIn engagement)](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
  - [Alice: Transitioning from supporting Marcel to Daniel (co-founder), handles invoices, contracts, CRM, sales follow-ups](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [Alice's role evolved significantly as the company grew (initially handled hiring, sales, invoices, follow-ups, coordination)](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)

### Tools and Systems

  - [Key tools: Linear, Notion, Zapier](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=451.0)
  - [CRM: RTO (Alice offered to add Dignory if needed)](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)
  - [Alice learned new tools on the job (e.g., RTO CRM)](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)

### Personal Backgrounds

  - [Dignory: Industrial engineering background, 3 years as EA/operations assistant/HR recruiter, learning automations/coding](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=63.0)
  - [Alice: Economics/finance background, 1 year as EA, pursuing master's degree](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=117.0)
  - [Dignory (25) from Colombia, Alice (28) from Kenya; 7-hour time difference](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)

### Work-Life Balance

  - [Alice balances work, studies (master's degree), and family (3 children)](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=954.0)
  - [Dignory practices martial arts, enjoys reading](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1007.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Dignory to reach out to Alice via Slack or WhatsApp for any questions or support](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [Alice to send an email to Dignory with her email signature for reference](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=595.0)
  - [No regular meetings scheduled; ad-hoc communication as needed](https://fathom.video/share/jQLyeaMcnxVkqBxTHuxqKBRPhhmqi7jZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1222.0)

action_items.description: Send email to Dignory with GrowthX signature for Dignory to copy
Set up GrowthX email signature based on Alice's template
Share WhatsApp number with Dignory via Slack (not note taker)
